artist pack · lullaby × dreamtime

axel
mansoor

lullaby × dreamtime  ·  mvmt studio, cascais  ·  tuesday, july 7 2026

what's lullaby club?

we're a community creating immersive musical experiences. our mission? to soothe the world through the power of music and vibe.

it started in 2021. lockdown. an artist named axel mansoor began singing people to sleep on clubhouse — and those late-night gatherings became lullaby club. it was a sleeper hit. (haha.)

then clubhouse made axel's face the app icon, and suddenly lullaby club was everywhere. for 250 nights in a row it sang the world to sleep: 400+ artists, a million-plus listeners, and the coziest corner of the internet — an award-winning community that merges the intimacy of a house concert with the playfulness of a whisper-filled blanket fort.

nap time is the new happy hour.

as featured in
  • The New York TimesMeet Axel Mansoor, the Clubhouse Whisperer →
  • PollstarKing of the Clubhouse: How Axel Mansoor Became the New Social Network's Breakout Music Star →
  • NPR · Here & NowCatch Some Tunes — and Zzzs — at Lullaby Club →

about axel

Axel Mansoor is an Emmy-nominated musician and the founder of Lullaby Club — the global phenomenon that sang millions of people to sleep, night after night, through the pandemic.

His live sets are one of a kind: part concert, part ceremony, part collective exhale — songs, soundscapes, and playful improvisation that guide rooms into an expansive softening.

the night

dream·time  |  the experience

  1. less a concert, more a collective wind-down: low light, a sea of blankets and cushions, and a voice in the dark.
  2. guitar, keys, and live soundscaping easing a room from waking toward rest.
  3. the lullaby club live experience, distilled — the coziness of the blanket fort and the intimacy of a house concert, with none of the festival noise.

two ways to run it

  • soloAxel performs the full set himself — voice, guitar, keys, and live soundscaping, start to finish.
  • hostedAxel curates and facilitates, performing alongside a few invited local musicians — lullaby-club style: a warm round of voices sharing one long wind-down. He holds the arc; the room does the rest.

either way: come horizontal. leave softer.

the setup (tech rider)

axel brings

  • laptop + audio interface — runs his own mix
  • SM58 dynamic vocal mic (has his own)
  • compact MIDI keyboard (runs into the laptop)
  • acoustic-electric guitar, stand & cable
  • loop station + effects

venue provides

  • a PA system (stereo, full-range)
  • a stereo feed into the PA (DI or line) from his interface — that's the main ask
  • power outlet(s) near the performance spot
  • low, ambient lighting — no harsh spotlight during the set

nice to have

  • 1× wedge monitor for vocals (ideal, not essential)
  • cool room temperature — guests are lying down (nice, not necessary)
  • water + tea at the stage

the room

  • guests rest on blankets & cushions — floor seating, low light
  • phones / recording: artist sets the tone with the room before the set
  • no loud re-entry once the set begins

Axel runs his own mix from a laptop + interface and brings his mic, guitar, and keys, so the footprint is small. The one real ask is a PA he can send a finished stereo feed into. Happy to sort the connection ahead of time.

event copy (for listings)

come lie down. axel mansoor — founder of lullaby club, the show that sang a million-plus people to sleep — brings that voice-in-the-dark into the room with you: live voice, guitar, keys, and layered soundscaping woven into one long exhale. part concert, part ceremony, part collective nap. nap time is the new happy hour. come rest into something.
